questions.text: Global variables are created in:

options.explanation: RAM (incorrect) stands for Random Access Memory. RAM is the computer's main memory and where all programs and data are stored while the computer is running. ROM (incorrect) stands for Read-Only Memory. ROM is a type of non-volatile memory that cannot be written to after it is manufactured. Global variables typically need to be changed during the program's execution, so ROM would not be suitable storage.

Cache is the most likely place where global variables are created. A cache is a temporary storage location that holds frequently accessed data. Global variables, by their nature, are likely to be accessed from multiple parts of a program, so caching them can improve program performance.

questions.text: Which of the following manages the allocation and de-allocation of memory for program variables?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: An IDE is a software application that provides comprehensive facilities for programmers to develop software. It may include features like code editing, compilation, debugging, and project management. IDEs typically don't directly manage memory allocation for program variables.

A compiler translates source code written in a programming language into machine code that the computer can understand. While the compiler may play a role in determining the memory requirements of a program, it doesn't directly manage memory allocation during program execution.

The operating system is responsible for managing system resources, including memory. When a program is loaded into memory, the OS allocates memory space for the program's code and data structures, including the memory for program variables. The OS also keeps track of which parts of memory are being used by different programs and deallocates memory when it's no longer needed.

Global variable is a variable that is declared outside of any function or block of code. It has global scope, meaning it can be accessed from anywhere in the program. Local variables are the opposite of global variables in terms of scope.

A static variable is a special type of local variable that retains its value between different calls to the function or block in which it's declared. While local variables are typically destroyed when the function exits, a static variable maintains its value
